vitals.terrain: mountainous roads with iconic Dolomite passes
vitals.avg_gradient: varies by timed section, typically steep climbs
vitals.aid_stations: feed zones on each stage (e.g., San Cipriano, Passo Costalunga)
vitals.time_limit: no overall time limit; group riding pace with timed sections
vitals.participation_trend: international field, steady popularity as historic event

course_description.overview: 5-stage amateur road cycling event over legendary Dolomite passes including Sellaronda and Stelvio area routes, with group riding and timed mountain time trials (crono sections) on traffic-free roads.

key_segments.name: Stage 1: Tires – Carezza
key_segments.description: Bolzano to Passo Nigra and Passo Costalunga; timed: 6.9 km, 542 m gain[1][3]

key_segments.name: Stage 2: Sellaronda
key_segments.description: Ponte Gardena loop over Passo Sella, Pordoi, Campolongo, Gardena; timed section ~6-16 km[1][2]

key_segments.name: Stage 3: Renon
key_segments.description: Bolzano to Collalbo; timed: 11.5 km, 791 m gain through vineyards and forest[1][3]

key_segments.name: Stage 4: Predazzo to Falcade via Passo Rolle
key_segments.description: Toughest stage into Trentino over Passo Rolle[2]

key_segments.name: Stage 5: Meltina – San Genesio
key_segments.description: Bolzano via Terlano to San Genesio; timed: 9 km, 789 m gain[1]

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Multi-day challenge with ~9,000 m total climbing across steep, iconic passes; mix of social group riding and competitive timed climbs up to 1,200 m gain.

logistics.getting_there: Fly to Bolzano Airport or Innsbruck/Milan; daily starts/ends at Bolzano Fair; trains/buses connect to South Tyrol.
logistics.accommodation: Bolzano base with hotels; bike-friendly options in South Tyrol; multi-day event allows central stay.
logistics.registration_details: Opens ~October prior; single stages available; international participants; limited to ~600 per stage[4][6].
logistics.bike_logistics: Road bikes recommended; traffic-free timed sections; support via group pace and feeds.
logistics.local_considerations: UNESCO Dolomites; July weather variable (warm days, cool highs); altitude effects on climbs.

final_verdict.one_liner: Historic 5-day Dolomites road epic blending social rides with brutal timed climbs.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Strong amateur climbers seeking iconic passes, UNESCO views, and international camaraderie.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for Dolomites road biking prestige and unique format.
pro_tips: Base in Bolzano for easy logistics.
Focus on cronos for GC contention.
Pack layers for high-altitude weather shifts.
